units.title: Listening — Office (Unit 1)
units.content:
## Listening focus: Office

Sample lines you may hear:

- A: Could you send the revised report by noon?
- B: Sure, I will email the PDF after I fix the charts.

Key vocabulary: deadline, attachment, invoice, reception, contract, calendar.

Strategy: listen for purpose, details, and signal words before choosing an answer.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: business people office meeting reviewing documents budget

options.A: The supervisor is printing the budget proposal.
options.B: The supervisor is approving the vacation request.
options.C: The supervisor is approving the budget proposal.
options.D: The supervisor is rejecting the budget proposal.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: team meeting conference room charts screen discussion

options.A: Colleagues are printing charts in the break room.
options.B: Colleagues are discussing emails in the conference room.
options.C: Colleagues are discussing charts in the conference room.
options.D: Colleagues are discussing charts in the parking lot.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: hotel reception desk visitor lobby concierge

options.A: The security guard is directing a visitor to the elevator.
options.B: The receptionist is directing a visitor to the cafeteria.
options.C: The receptionist is directing a visitor to the elevator.
options.D: The receptionist is directing a package to the elevator.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: office mail sorting workplace printer copier

options.A: Staff are sorting mail trays near the copy machine.
options.B: Staff are sorting packages near the copy machine.
options.C: Staff are shredding mail trays near the copy machine.
options.D: Staff are sorting mail trays near the exit door.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: person typing laptop computer desk office workspace

options.A: A manager is typing on a laptop at a tidy workstation.
options.B: An employee is sleeping on a laptop at a tidy workstation.
options.C: An employee is typing on a phone at a tidy workstation.
options.D: An employee is typing on a laptop at a tidy workstation.

questions.sentence: The meeting will start at nine AM _____ room three B.
We need _____ schedule a follow-up call next Monday.
She submitted _____ invoice to the accounting department.
He is _____ the contract with the legal team.
The printer _____ the second floor is out of paper.
Please forward the attachment before the _____ today.
Please cc your manager _____ all client emails.

questions.question: In the office context from this unit, which vocabulary word fits best: "We need to confirm the _____ before the client call."

options.A: attachment
options.B: reception
options.C: deadline
options.D: printer

options.A: department

options.A: department

options.A: reception

units.title: Listening — Meeting (Unit 2)
units.content:
## Listening focus: Meeting

Sample lines you may hear:

- A: Let's move the stand-up to nine thirty.
- B: Works for me; I will update the calendar invite.

Key vocabulary: agenda, minutes, mute, quarterly, sprint, participant.

Strategy: listen for purpose, details, and signal words before choosing an answer.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: video conference call laptop remote meeting screen

options.A: Participants are joining a phone call on laptops.
options.B: Participants are ending a video call on laptops.
options.C: Participants are joining a video call on laptops.
options.D: Participants are joining a video call on tablets.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: business presentation meeting projector screen charts

options.A: The presenter is pointing at a slide with vacation photos.
options.B: The presenter is pointing at a whiteboard with quarterly figures.
options.C: The presenter is pointing at a slide with quarterly figures.
options.D: The attendee is pointing at a slide with quarterly figures.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: business handshake agreement deal office meeting

options.A: Two managers are shaking hands before reading the agreement.
options.B: Two managers are waving goodbye after signing the agreement.
options.C: Two interns are shaking hands after signing the agreement.
options.D: Two managers are shaking hands after signing the agreement.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: conference audience taking notes seminar workshop

options.A: Attendees are writing notes during the lunch break.
options.B: Attendees are writing notes during the Q and A session.
options.C: Speakers are writing notes during the Q and A session.
options.D: Attendees are typing emails during the Q and A session.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: team brainstorming sticky notes whiteboard workshop agile

options.A: Clients are brainstorming with sticky notes on a glass wall.
options.B: Team members are shredding sticky notes on a glass wall.
options.C: Team members are brainstorming with sticky notes on a glass wall.
options.D: Team members are brainstorming with sticky notes on the floor.

questions.sentence: We will table _____ topic until the next sprint review.
The quarterly targets were discussed _____ detail.
The video link was sent _____ all participants yesterday.
Could everyone mute their microphones _____ not speaking?
The agenda was shared ten minutes before _____ call.
The minutes will _____ circulated by end of day Friday.
Please raise your _____ in the chat if you agree.

options.A: sprint
options.B: quarterly
options.C: review
options.D: participant

units.title: Listening — Travel (Unit 3)
units.content:
## Listening focus: Travel

Sample lines you may hear:

- A: Is this gate for the delayed flight to Denver?
- B: Yes, boarding should begin in twenty minutes.

Key vocabulary: boarding, platform, checkpoint, shuttle, turbulence, terminal.

Strategy: listen for purpose, details, and signal words before choosing an answer.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: airport departure board flight information traveler

options.A: A traveler is checking a departure board at the train station.
options.B: A traveler is checking a departure board at the airport.
options.C: A staff member is checking a departure board at the airport.
options.D: A traveler is checking a luggage tag at the airport.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: airport gate boarding queue passengers waiting

options.A: Passengers are sitting in line at the gate.
options.B: Passengers are waiting in line at the gate.
options.C: Crew members are waiting in line at the gate.
options.D: Passengers are waiting in line at the taxi stand.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: airport check-in luggage suitcase airline counter

options.A: The agent is tagging carry-on luggage at the counter.
options.B: The passenger is tagging checked luggage at the counter.
options.C: The agent is tagging checked luggage at the counter.
options.D: The agent is weighing checked luggage at the security line.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: tourist traveler map city navigation street

options.A: A tourist is folding a map near the hotel entrance.
options.B: A concierge is reading a map near the hotel entrance.
options.C: A tourist is reading a menu near the hotel entrance.
options.D: A tourist is reading a map near the hotel entrance.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: traveler walking suitcase luggage airport hotel hallway

options.A: A traveler is running with a rolling suitcase along a bright corridor.
options.B: A traveler is walking with a rolling suitcase along a bright corridor.
options.C: A traveler is walking without luggage along a bright corridor.
options.D: A pilot is walking with a rolling suitcase along a bright corridor.

questions.sentence: The rental car desk is located in _____ two.
Passengers must show _____ at the security checkpoint.
The _____ shuttle leaves from the main entrance hourly.
Heavy traffic is _____ near the airport exit.
Your boarding pass is available _____ the airline app.
Your connecting flight arrives in _____ at six PM.
The train departs from platform twelve _____ ten minutes.

options.A: checkpoint

options.C: connecting
options.D: platform

units.title: Listening — Restaurant (Unit 4)
units.content:
## Listening focus: Restaurant

Sample lines you may hear:

- A: Could we have a table for four near the window?
- B: Of course; your server will be right with you.

Key vocabulary: reservation, sparkling, dessert, grilled, special, kitchen.

Strategy: listen for purpose, details, and signal words before choosing an answer.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: restaurant server dining table guests meal service

options.A: A server is pouring water for guests at the table.
options.B: A server is pouring wine for guests at the table.
options.C: A server is pouring water for staff at the counter.
options.D: A host is pouring water for guests at the table.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: chef cooking kitchen restaurant food plating

options.A: The chef is washing a dish in the kitchen.
options.B: The chef is plating a dish in the kitchen.
options.C: The waiter is plating a dish in the kitchen.
options.D: The chef is plating a dish at the bar.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: restaurant interior dining menu cozy lighting

options.A: Customers are ordering menus under soft lighting.
options.B: Customers are reading menus under bright sunlight.
options.C: Customers are reading menus under soft lighting.
options.D: Customers are reading newspapers under soft lighting.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: restaurant window table couple dining host

options.A: The host is billing a couple by the window.
options.B: The host is seating a couple by the kitchen.
options.C: The host is seating a couple by the window.
options.D: The server is seating a couple by the window.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: friends dinner table sharing food restaurant group

options.A: Friends are sharing plates at an empty counter.
options.B: Friends are packing plates at a crowded dinner table.
options.C: Strangers are sharing plates at a crowded dinner table.
options.D: Friends are sharing plates at a crowded dinner table.

questions.sentence: Would _____ like still or sparkling water with that?
Could we split _____ bill three ways, please?
The daily special includes soup and a _____ salad.
The kitchen can _____ the dish without nuts.
The dessert menu is _____ the last page.
May I take your order _____ you are ready?

units.title: Listening — Shopping (Unit 5)
units.content:
## Listening focus: Shopping

Sample lines you may hear:

- A: Do you have this jacket in medium?
- B: Let me check stock in the back for you.

Key vocabulary: loyalty, checkout, fitting, receipt, returns, sale.

Strategy: listen for purpose, details, and signal words before choosing an answer.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: clothing store shopping shirts rack retail fashion

options.A: A shopper is comparing two shirts on a rack.
options.B: A shopper is comparing two shoes on a rack.
options.C: A shopper is folding two shirts on a rack.
options.D: A cashier is comparing two shirts on a rack.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: supermarket checkout cashier scanning groceries

options.A: The cashier is scanning items at the checkout lane.
options.B: The cashier is bagging items at the checkout lane.
options.C: The customer is scanning items at the checkout lane.
options.D: The cashier is scanning items at the fitting room.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: customer service desk help counter store support

options.A: A manager is asking about a warranty at the service desk.
options.B: A customer is asking about a discount at the service desk.
options.C: A customer is asking about a warranty at the service desk.
options.D: A customer is asking about a warranty at the food court.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: shopping mall bags walking corridor retail

options.A: Shoppers are carrying bags through the mall corridor.
options.B: Staff are carrying bags through the mall corridor.
options.C: Shoppers are carrying bags through the parking garage.
options.D: Shoppers are carrying carts through the mall corridor.

questions.stem: Listen and choose the best description of the situation.
questions.image_query: grocery store vegetables fruits aisle shopping cart

options.A: A customer is choosing produce in a well-lit grocery aisle.
options.B: A cashier is choosing produce in a well-lit grocery aisle.
options.C: A customer is choosing produce in a dark parking lot.
options.D: A customer is returning produce in a well-lit grocery aisle.

questions.sentence: Your loyalty points will _____ applied at checkout.
We _____ free gift wrapping during the holiday season.
You can return _____ items within thirty days.
This _____ is on sale until the end of the weekend.
The electronics department is _____ the third floor.
Would you like a receipt sent to your _____ address?
Cash or card payments are accepted _____ every register.
